item.title: About
item.author: Unknown
item.description:
So far, what you'll find here is produced by Flynn, who was a deep generalist before the term existed and has worked in computing since 1983. He's pretty much run the gamut from the kernel up through distributed applications (with a common thread of communications and security throughout) as well as working roles from solo coder up through Silicon Valley VP.

Since 2022, he's been a tech evangelist at Buoyant, educating developers about Linkerd, Kubernetes, and cloud-native development in general. He's a coauthor of Linkerd: Up and Running from O'Reilly, a chair of the CNCF AI Gateway Working Group, a co-lead of Gateway API for mesh, and the original author and longest-serving maintainer of the Emissary-ingress API gateway.

He is most easily found as @flynn on the CNCF or Linkerd Slacks.

item.title: "AI" is a Terrible Name
item.author: Unknown
item.description:
Finkle-McGraw brightened a bit. "You know, when I was a lad they called it A.I. Artificial intelligence."

Hackworth allowed himself a tight, narrow, and brief smile. "Well, there's something to be said for cheekiness, I suppose."

-- Neal Stephenson, The Diamond Age, 1995

item.title: On Shipping Software
item.author: Unknown
item.description: There are two kinds of software: software that ships, and software that's irrelevant.

item.title: On Scaling Nodes and Startup Races
item.author: Unknown
item.description: What really happens when all your nodes die?

item.title: The Comment Manifesto
item.author: Unknown
item.description: If this code ships and your comments aren't in it, you'll regret it -- maybe not today, maybe not tomorrow, but soon, and for the rest of your life.
